title: Proto-Nago: The First Signs of Survival
status: PRESENT — transcribed directly from PLATE_PROTO_NAGO.png (visual source of truth)
framing_text: Before Nago was a language, it was a desperate need. Made by hands that had no common words left. Made for shelter, for runs, for strangers, for help. Made to be seen, felt, and understood. Not perfect. Not beautiful. Necessary. This is where Nago begins.
closing_quote: We had no words. So we made signs. We had no safety. So we marked the way. We had no certainty. So we watched each other. We had each other. That was enough.
made_with_what_we_had: Anything — if it could mark, it became Nago.
not_perfect_still_understood_note: Many hands. Many styles. One meaning. — variant renderings of the same mark were all readable as the same sign.
promise_of_nago_proto: One day these marks will become a language. One day they will be beautiful. But they will never forget their beginning. Nago was born in struggle. Nago was born in care. Nago was born for Umada. And Nago will always belong to the people.

marks.name: Bread / Food
marks.meaning: Food here. Share if you can.

open_questions: Whether the seven marks not carried into the refined eight (Go This Way, Care/Love, Look/Watch, Gather, Down/Below, Up/Above, Stop/No) were retired, absorbed into a function family some other way, or are simply awaiting a refined re-render — not stated on either plate.
Rest does not appear as a distinct Proto-Nago mark; its refined-layer meaning may descend from Safe or Home, or may be a later addition — not stated on the proto plate.
